task_completion.description: Navigate to the Rocket.Chat login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat login screen is visible in a web browser or desktop application.

task_completion.description: Successfully log in to Rocket.Chat as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Rocket.Chat workspace interface is visible, showing the sidebar with channels and the main chat area, indicating a successful login.
The '#release-updates' channel is highlighted in the left sidebar and the channel name is visible in the top header of the main chat area.

task_completion.description: Add a white check mark reaction to the oldest message
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ✅ (white check mark) emoji reaction is visible attached to the bottom of the oldest/first announcement message in the channel's history.

task_completion.description: Add an eyes reaction to the newest message
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 👀 (eyes) emoji reaction is visible attached to the bottom of the newest/latest announcement message in the channel's history.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the Rocket.Chat web or desktop UI, rather than a terminal window running API scripts or curl commands.

integrity.description: Emojis were added as actual message reactions, not standalone messages
integrity.visual_evidence: The emojis appear as small reaction badges attached directly to the announcement messages, not as new text messages sent by the admin user in the chat feed.

integrity.description: Results appear to come from genuine software interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface looks like standard Rocket.Chat without browser developer tools open to manipulate the DOM or fake the reactions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target channel: 'release-updates'. Expected reaction for the oldest message: ✅ (:white_check_mark:). Expected reaction for the newest message: 👀 (:eyes:).
